flavorText: Open Hand monks specialize in precision control: rider pressure, self-sustain, and a lethal setup finisher.

features.description: Your Flurry of Blows gains control riders that can push, drop, or disrupt enemies, turning basic monk pressure into a battlefield-shaping toolkit.
As an action, you draw on inner discipline to restore your own vitality, giving the subclass a reliable self-heal that keeps you in the fight.
Your centered presence makes it harder for enemies to commit violence against you until they break the calm, creating strong pre-fight and control value.
You plant a devastating delayed vibration in a foe and can later trigger it for massive internal damage, giving the subclass a signature high-tier finisher.

open-hand-open-hand-technique.name: Open Hand Technique
open-hand-open-hand-technique.summary: Your Flurry of Blows can impose rider effects (push, prone, or reaction denial), turning strikes into battlefield control.
open-hand-open-hand-technique.description: When you use Flurry of Blows, each unarmed hit can apply control riders such as push, knock prone, or reaction denial (save as appropriate to your table rules).
open-hand-open-hand-technique.trigger: When your Flurry of Blows hits.
open-hand-open-hand-technique.usage: Applied through Flurry of Blows (typically costs 1 Focus Point).

open-hand-wholeness-of-body.name: Wholeness of Body
open-hand-wholeness-of-body.summary: Use disciplined breathing and inner focus to restore your own vitality mid-adventure or mid-fight.
open-hand-wholeness-of-body.description:
As an action, you draw on inner discipline to restore your own vitality, giving the subclass a reliable self-heal that keeps you in the fight.

Wholeness of Body is a real sustain tool for the Open Hand monk. It matters because self-healing changes whether you can stay aggressive without immediate support.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.
open-hand-wholeness-of-body.usage: 1 action to restore hit points to yourself.

open-hand-tranquility.name: Tranquility
open-hand-tranquility.summary: Your centered presence discourages violence until enemies deliberately break the peace around you.
open-hand-tranquility.description:
Your centered presence makes it harder for enemies to commit violence against you until they break the calm, creating strong pre-fight and control value.

Tranquility is about control before the first punch lands. It should stand out in scouting, negotiation, and tense pre-combat scenes where initiative has not yet exploded.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.
open-hand-tranquility.duration: Until broken or as defined by table rules.
open-hand-tranquility.effect: Gain Sanctuary-like protection until you attack, cast a harmful effect, or the protection is broken.

open-hand-quivering-palm.name: Quivering Palm
open-hand-quivering-palm.summary: Plant a hidden lethal vibration in a target and trigger it later for a terrifying finishing burst.
open-hand-quivering-palm.description:
You plant a devastating delayed vibration in a foe and can later trigger it for massive internal damage, giving the subclass a signature high-tier finisher.

Quivering Palm is the Open Hand monk’s signature endgame finisher. It should feel like a major setup-and-payoff mechanic, not a one-line damage rider.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.
open-hand-quivering-palm.trigger: After hitting with an unarmed strike; later triggered with an action.
open-hand-quivering-palm.usage: Spend Focus to plant the vibration on a hit, then use an action later to trigger it.
open-hand-quivering-palm.effect: Target makes a Constitution save; on failure it drops to 0 hit points or suffers massive necrotic/internal damage.
